description: Pre-Session B classification for desire (reg 43). Confirms anchor Luk 22:15 / Gal 5:16. Restores 5 deleted cha.mad terms to extracted — their delete status is incorrectly applied (the notes say 'Bleed: desire registry' but they ARE in the desire registry). Extracts 7 None-status terms. Confirms G4658 skuthēs candidate_delete (place-holder/ethnic name, not desire vocabulary). Documents H0157G xref_desire status and reading scope.

operations.description: Confirm dual anchor: Luk 22:15 ('I have earnestly desired to eat this Passover with you before I suffer' — divine desire, epithumia epethumesa double form) + Gal 5:16 ('walk by the Spirit and you will not gratify the desires of the flesh' — two-forms structure). Together they frame desire as the inner motivating force that can be oriented toward God (Luk 22:15) or toward the flesh (Gal 5:16).

H2530A cha.mad [to desire/covet] — restore delete → extracted. 20 verses. The 10th commandment verb (Exo 20:17: 'you shall not covet'). Gen 3:6 (tree desirable to the eyes). Psa 68:16 (God desired Zion for his abode). Pro 6:25 (do not desire her beauty). Isa 53:2 (no form or majesty that we should desire him). Mic 2:2 (they covet fields). The delete note reads 'Bleed: desire registry (reg 43)' which is CIRCULAR — cha.mad IS primary desire vocabulary and belongs in this registry. Delete status was misapplied.

set.status_note: Restored from delete. Primary covet/desire verb. Exo 20:17 (10th commandment), Gen 3:6, Psa 68:16, Isa 53:2.

operations.description: H2530B cha.mu.dah [precious/desirable] — restore delete → extracted. 20 verses (same pool as H2530A). Precious/desirable adjective form of cha.mad. Dan 9:23 — Daniel 'greatly beloved/desired' (cha.mudat). Song 2:3 — 'with great delight I sat in his shadow.' Isa 53:2 — 'no form that we should desire him'. Shares verse pool with H2530A — the sub-gloss covers the adjectival/nominal form.

set.status_note: Restored from delete. Adjectival/nominal form of cha.mad. Precious/desirable things. Same verse pool as H2530A.

operations.description: H2531 che.med [delight/desirability] — restore delete → extracted. 6 verses. Isa 27:2 (pleasant vineyard), Isa 32:12 (pleasant fields), Eze 23:6,12,23 (desirable young men — the desire vocabulary in the Oholah/Oholibah allegory of Israel's disordered desire), Amo 5:11 (houses of hewn stone). The Eze 23 uses are analytically significant — desirable men as the object of Israel's illicit covenantal desire.

set.status_note: Restored from delete. Delight/desirability noun. Eze 23 uses are the covenantal-infidelity desire vocabulary.

operations.description: H2532A chem.dah [desired/precious thing] — restore delete → extracted. 16 verses. Jer 3:19 ('a pleasant land, a heritage most beautiful of all nations' — God's desired gift to Israel), Dan 11:37 ('the thing beloved by women' — a desired deity), Hag 2:7 ('the treasures of all nations shall come in'), Jer 12:10 ('pleasant portion'). The chem.dah vocabulary covers precious/desirable things — the objects of desire. Distinct from cha.mad (the act of desiring).

set.status_note: Restored from delete. Precious/desirable things vocabulary. Jer 3:19, Dan 11:37, Hag 2:7.

operations.description: H4261 mach.mad [precious thing/desire] — restore delete → extracted. 13 verses. Song 5:16 — 'he is altogether desirable (mach.mad). This is my beloved.' The most concentrated single desire-word in the Song. Eze 24:16,21,25 — God will take away the prophet's delight (mach.mad eyes), the temple as the people's mach.mad. Lam 1:10,11 — enemy seized her precious things (mach.mad). The loss of what is desired as judgment.

set.status_note: Restored from delete. Precious/desired thing. Song 5:16 (altogether desirable), Eze 24:16 (delight of your eyes), Lam 1:10 (precious things seized).

operations.description: H2836A cha.shaq [to desire] → extracted. Intense desire/attachment. Deu 7:7 — 'the Lord set his love on you' (cha.shaq —

set.status_note: Intense desire/attachment. Deu 7:7 — 'the Lord set his love on you' (cha.shaq — divine desire as choosing Israel). Deu 10:15 — 'the Lord set his heart in love on your fathers.' Psa 91:14 — 'because he holds fast to me in love.' Deu 21:11 — desire for a captive woman. Gen 34:8 — 'the soul of my son S

operations.description: H2836B cha.shaq [to connect] → extracted. Sub-gloss of H2836A. Same verse pool (8 verses). The connection/attachment sense

set.status_note: Sub-gloss of H2836A. Same verse pool (8 verses). The connection/attachment sense of cha.shaq — desire as the bond that connects. Deu 7:7 and Deu 10:15 feature the divine cha.shaq for Israel as both desire and covenantal attachment.

operations.description: H2837 che.sheq [desire] → extracted. Noun form of cha.shaq. 1Ki 9:1 — 'all that Solomon desired to do.' 1Ki 9:19 / 2C

set.status_note: Noun form of cha.shaq. 1Ki 9:1 — 'all that Solomon desired to do.' 1Ki 9:19 / 2Ch 8:6 — cities for all Solomon's desires. Isa 21:4 — 'the twilight I longed for has been turned into trembling.' Desire as the planned goal of action.

operations.description: H3700 ka.saph [to long] → extracted. Intense longing. Gen 31:30 — 'you longed greatly for your father's house.' Job 1

set.status_note: Intense longing. Gen 31:30 — 'you longed greatly for your father's house.' Job 14:15 — 'you would long for the work of your hands' — God's longing for his creature. Psa 84:2 — 'my soul longs, yes faints for the courts of the Lord.' Psa 17:12 — like a lion eager to tear. Zep 2:1 — gather together (in

operations.description: G3713 oregō [to aspire] → extracted. To reach/aspire toward. 1Ti 3:1 — 'if anyone aspires to the office of overseer,

set.status_note: To reach/aspire toward. 1Ti 3:1 — 'if anyone aspires to the office of overseer, he desires a noble task.' 1Ti 6:10 — 'through this craving some have wandered away.' Heb 11:16 — 'they desire a better country, that is, a heavenly one.' The upward-reaching desire vocabulary — aspiration toward a goal.

operations.description: H5690 a.gav [lust] → extracted. Licentious desire/lust. Eze 33:31-32 — 'they hear what you say but do not do it.

set.status_note: Licentious desire/lust. Eze 33:31-32 — 'they hear what you say but do not do it... you are to them like one who sings lustful songs.' The lustful-song context is the desire vocabulary at its most disordered — desire divorced from reality and response. Related to H5691 a.ga.vah (lust) in the love reg

operations.description: H4262 mach.mod [desirable] → extracted. Desirable/precious. Lam 1:7 — 'Jerusalem remembers... all the precious things (m

set.status_note: Desirable/precious. Lam 1:7 — 'Jerusalem remembers... all the precious things (mach.mad) that were hers.' Variant form of H4261 mach.mad. Desire vocabulary in the loss context — precious things remembered after they are taken.

operations.description: H0157G a.hev [to love: lover] — xref_desire status confirmed. Note from prior Session B analysis: primary love verb with 184 verses (207 occurrences). Session B v3.0 note states primary analysis is held in desire registry (reg 43) with cross-ref to love (reg 103) via PH2-103-001. All 184 verses have been read. This term's desire-dimension (love as the supreme desire) is treated in Session B analysis for desire registry. Session D cross-registry resolution required between love (103) and desire (43) for this term.

set.xref_note: H0157G a.hev read with all 1,717 verses. Desire-as-love dimension analysed. Session D resolution needed.

operations.description: Confirm candidate_delete → delete for 110 terms. Key confirmations: G4658 skuthēs (Scythian ethnic name, not desire vocabulary — entered via Col 3:11 context), H4057B mid.bar (wilderness), H5375H na.sa:bear (carry vocabulary), H3423H ya.rash:take (possession vocabulary), G1905 eperōtaō (to question — different from erōtaō to ask), H1696I da.var:promise (speech vocabulary), H2654A cha.phets:to delight (covered by H2656 che.phets extracted), H7521 ra.tsah:to accept (covered by H7522 ra.tson extracted), H1934 ha.va:to be (Aramaic existence verb), H0160 a.ha.vah:love (primary in love registry), H5315 ne.phesh sub-glosses (soul vocabulary — covered in soul registry), G1012 boulē:plan (covered by G1014 boulomai and G1013 boulēma extracted), G2106 eudokeō:to delight (covered by G2107 eudokia extracted), G0700 areskō:to please (covered by G0701 arestos extracted).

set.exclusion_reason: Confirmed candidate_delete. Bleed vocabulary or adequately covered by extracted terms.

operations.description: Set remaining None-status terms without verses → delete. Bleed vocabulary: H1697G da.var (word), G3844 para (preposition), G2564G kaleō (to call), H3701G ke.seph (silver), H0176A o (or), H5315 ne.phesh sub-glosses G-N (soul/appetite vocabulary — covered in soul registry), G1012 boulē (covered by extracted boulomai/boulēma), H5375J na.sa:guilt, H0160 a.ha.vah:love (primary in love registry), H7613 se.et (elevation), H6440 pa.neh sub-glosses (face), G1380 dokeō (to think), G2095 eu (well/well done), G3874 paraklēsis (encouragement — covered by parakaleō extracted), H7521 ra.tsah (accept — covered by ra.tson extracted), H2654A cha.phets (delight — covered by che.phets extracted), H1934 ha.va (to be, Aramaic).

set.exclusion_reason: Bleed vocabulary or adequately covered by extracted terms.

summary.anchor_confirmed: Luk 22:15 (divine desire — double epithumia form); Gal 5:16 (two-forms structure: flesh vs Spirit desire)
new_extractions_from_none: H2836A cha.shaq (desire, 8v) — divine desire for Israel, Deu 7:7
G3713 oregō (to aspire, 3v) — Heb 11:16 heavenly country
summary.reading_scope: 1,717 verses across 56 terms (43 extracted + 7 None-status + 1 xref_desire + 5 restored-delete)
summary.key_finding: The cha.mad family (Exo 20:17 10th commandment vocabulary) was incorrectly deleted. Restored. H2836A cha.shaq establishes divine desire for Israel (Deu 7:7) as a primary desire-vocabulary term alongside the human coveting vocabulary.
